content.Text: [Mon 2026-04-20 03:37 PDT] Create a new interactive HTML tool at docs/graph-compare.html for GraphVisual — a "Graph Comparison Tool" that lets users compare two graphs side by side with structural similarity analysis. ## What to build A single self-contained HTML file (docs/graph-compare.html) that: 1. **Two side-by-side Canvas panels** — each renders a graph (Graph A and Graph B) with drag-to-move nodes 2. **Input methods** for each graph: - Text adjacency list input (e.g. "0-1, 0-2, 1-3") - 8 presets: Petersen, K4, K3,3, Cube (Q3), Cycle-6, Star-5, Path-5, Random(8,12) 3. **Metrics comparison table** showing both graphs' metrics side by side: - Nodes, Edges, Density, Avg Degree, Max Degree, Min Degree, Diameter, Radius, Components, Clustering Coefficient, Is Bipartite, Chromatic Number (greedy upper bound) 4. **Similarity scoring** with 3 methods: - Degree sequence similarity (sort degree sequences, compare with normalized distance) - Spectral similarity (compare eigenvalues of adjacency matrices — compute top-k eigenvalues, measure L2 distance) - Edit distance estimate (simple heuristic based on node/edge count differences + degree distribution divergence) - Overall similarity score (weighted average, 0-100%) 5. **Visual diff highlighting**: - Color-code nodes by degree difference from the "other" graph's degree distribution - Show a degree distribution comparison bar chart below the canvases 6. **Export** — JSON export of comparison results ## Style requirements - Use the existing styles.css (link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css") - Follow the exact same sidebar navigation structure as other tools in docs/. Copy the sidebar from docs/dominating-set.html but add a new entry for this tool: `⚖️Graph Compare` in the Tools section - Dark theme using CSS variables (--surface, --bg, --border, --text, --accent, etc.) - Same .btn, .btn-primary, .btn-secondary, .control-group patterns - Responsive layout with grid ## Technical notes - Pure HTML/CSS/JS, no external dependencies - For spectral similarity, compute eigenvalues using QR algorithm or power iteration on the adjacency matrix (keep it simple — top 5 eigenvalues is fine) - For clustering coefficient, use the standard local clustering coefficient averaged over all nodes - Canvas rendering with force-directed layout (simple spring-embedder) - All computation client-side ## Also update docs/index.html Add a link/card for the Graph Compare tool in the index page's tool listing, following the existing pattern. ## Verification After creating the file, verify the HTML is well-formed by checking it has matching opening/closing tags for html, head, body, script.
